title: Revert "intel_alm: direct M10K instantiation"
body:
So, the M10K PR broke the Quartus backend in two ways, one trivial, one substantially less so.

- the `port_[ab]_last_address` parameters to `cyclonev_ram_block` use `CFG_DBITS` instead of `CFG_ABITS`; this means Quartus can fail to infer the RAM size and then gives up. This by itself is just a single character fix and a regression test to avoid breaking it again.
- all the RAM cells have the same logical RAM name, which worked fine in my own testing until I ran it with a different codebase and it broke horribly because Quartus does *not* like RAMs of different geometries having the same logical RAM name.

The problem is that Yosys does not have a way to assign a unique identifier to a cell parameter - even just the cell name would work, I believe. I've been wondering if it would be better to put it in `techmap` (which is already very complicated), or in `chparam`.

While I come up with an acceptable way to solve the logical RAM name problem, the PR should be reverted as to limit the breakage in master.
